headline: Mastercard (MA) Weighs Selling Vocalink Back To British Banks
summary: Mastercard (NYSE:MA) is reported to be considering the sale of a majority stake in its UK payments infrastructure subsidiary, Vocalink. The potential buyers are the British banks that previously owned Vocalink, amid ongoing national debate about foreign control of critical financial infrastructure. The discussions relate to the role and ownership of the systems that underpin much of the UK's retail payments activity. Vocalink sits at the core of the UK's payments system, processing...

headline: ⁠JP Morgan Just Upgraded The Stock It Thinks Is Most Shielded From Geopolitical Crises
summary: Geopolitical crises often create winners and losers in the stock market, but for a certain set of investors, investing remains more about surviving the uncertainties. The conflict in Iran is one such crisis that has significantly disrupted global supply chains, forcing even the most durable of American businesses to deal with delays and uncertain prices. […]

headline: Mizuho downgrades Circle on competitive threat from new rival stablecoin
summary: Investing.com -- Mizuho Securities downgraded Circle Internet Group, the issuer of the USDC stablecoin, to Underperform from Neutral and trimmed its price target to $50 from $85, flagging competitive threats from a newly launched rival stablecoin that could undercut Circle’s core revenue model.

headline: Ripple Is Now a Premier Member of the x402 Foundation, Alongside Visa and Mastercard
summary: AI agents are about to start spending money on their own, and whoever owns the rails they pay on owns a brand-new market. Ripple just took a seat at the table where those rules are being written, alongside Visa, Mastercard, and Google.

headline: Citigroup Q2 Earnings Call Highlights
summary: Citigroup (NYSE:C) reported a stronger second quarter of 2026, with management pointing to broad-based revenue growth, improved returns and continued capital returns, while cautioning that second-half results could be affected by normal seasonality and a deliberate increase in investment spending.

content.title: Citigroup Q2 Earnings Call Highlights
content.summary: Citigroup (NYSE:C) reported a stronger second quarter of 2026, with management pointing to broad-based revenue growth, improved returns and continued capital returns, while cautioning that second-half results could be affected by normal seasonality and a deliberate increase in investment spending.
